steps.title: Keep title with noun
steps.content: The quoted title "Little Marie" directly identifies the hit song. No punctuation is needed between a noun and the title that identifies it.

steps.title: Reject interrupting punctuation
steps.content: B inserts an unnecessary comma between "song" and its title. C uses a colon after a phrase that is not an independent clause. D uses a semicolon, which cannot connect a noun phrase to its title. No punctuation fits. The answer is A.
